message:
feat(fnp-python): expose trapezoid + trapz pyo3 bindings

Adds pyo3 entry points for numpy.trapezoid (numpy 2.0+) and its
pre-2.0 alias numpy.trapz, both routed to the underlying
UFuncArray::trapezoid / trapezoid_x implementations in fnp-ufunc.

crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:

- New trapezoid_impl(py, name, y, x, dx, axis) helper that
  extracts the y and optional x arrays through
  extract_numeric_array, dispatches to UFuncArray::trapezoid or
  trapezoid_x depending on whether x was provided, maps ufunc
  errors through map_ufunc_error, and materialises the result
  as either a numpy scalar or ndarray via
  build_numpy_scalar_or_array_from_ufunc so the Python side
  sees the same return-type rules numpy uses (scalar for 0-D
  inputs, ndarray otherwise).
- New #[pyfunction] trapezoid(y, x=None, dx=1.0, axis=-1) with
  pyo3 signature matching numpy.trapezoid exactly.
- Also registers a trapz(...) alias pointing at the same impl
  for compatibility with code written against numpy < 2.0.

.beads/issues.jsonl:

- br sync --flush-only covering the trapezoid / trapz binding
  task. Pure status transitions.
